summary: S5 (final, 5 of 5) shipped consumer rollout + docs + dual version bumps and performed the cross-provider verification of the S2-S5 deferred work. (1) docs/cross-repo-migration-guard-notice.md: paste-in for consumer CLAUDE.md -- one-time setup (install the SessionStart drift guard via the extension's dabbler.installOrchestratorHook.claudeCode command OR a router-less manual fallback = invoker raw-URL download + minimal settings.json stanza; raise the >=0.10.0 pin; run a one-time check_migrations + bulk-upgrade-on-drift), a self-contained copy-snippet (machine-enforced schema currency, raw GitHub URLs, schemaVersion:4 stamp, omit-null, old-schema-is-OK no-nag posture, NNN- number-prefix addressing with resolve_set/--next), and a 2026-05-29 adoption-status table. (2) Harvester first-adopter confirmed: all 49 sets at v4 (0 needs-migration), pin dabbler-ai-router>=0.10.0, CLAUDE.md stamped + raw-URL guidance -- all done ahead of the set; the remaining hook install is a one-time operator action against the SHARED GLOBAL ~/.claude/settings.json (the invoker walks up from cwd) which covers every repo on the machine at once. (3) Version bumps: dabbler-ai-router 0.11.0 -> 0.12.0 (pyproject.toml + ai_router/__init__.__version__) and extension 0.24.1 -> 0.25.0 (package.json; publishes the held 0.24.1 Copy-Slug fix); ai_router/CHANGELOG.md [0.12.0] release header (S4 resolve_set + S2 check_migrations/manifest), extension CHANGELOG.md [0.25.0] entry, CLAUDE.md Extension-versioning walk (new Current v0.25.0; Previous/Pre-Previous chain cascaded down one level), and a set-level change-log.md. Cross-provider verification: gemini-pro (google) IV&V via providers.call_model with the provider-scoped config (full cfg lacks a top-level 'retry' key; this also sidesteps the RouteResult.provider trap); first call truncated on thinking with max_tokens=4000 -> fixed with max_tokens=16000 + thinking_budget=4096. Verdict VERIFIED: 0 critical; 2 important (both NOTED RISKS not defects: three-step bulk-migration safety relies on each migrator's idempotency = already a documented/test-asserted invariant; Python/TS resolver dual-impl drift = acknowledged, convention-lint a future-set candidate); 1 nice-to-have (the 2 pre-existing Set-026 TS stub-harness failures, out of scope/predate the set). The verifier explicitly confirmed the empirically-corrected THREE-migrator bulk chain (vs the S1 verdict's two) as sound and called release discipline sound. No in-flight fix required; record at s5-verification.md. Tests: full Python suite 1025 passed / 1 skipped / 0 regressions after the bumps; no TS source changed in S5 (version + CHANGELOG only; S4 already confirmed tsc/esbuild clean). Cost $0.0053 -> cumulative routed $0.2882 of $10 NTE (2.9%). Publishes HELD for operator-initiated tag-push (vsix-v0.25.0 via publish-vscode.yml; v0.12.0 via release.yml) -- NOTE: VSCE_PAT was expired 2026-05-28, confirm freshness before the vsix tag. This is the final session; close_session flips the set's top-level status to complete.

reason.specifics: Final session of Set 050 -- no next session. Set complete: close_session flips the top-level status to complete. Operator follow-ups (out of session scope): (1) push the held publish tags vsix-v0.25.0 (Marketplace) and v0.12.0 (PyPI) after confirming VSCE_PAT freshness (expired 2026-05-28); (2) run the one-time SessionStart drift-guard hook install (extension command) which covers all repos via the shared global ~/.claude/settings.json; (3) paste the cross-repo-migration-guard-notice.md snippet into the remaining consumer repos' CLAUDE.md (operator-scoped). Sets 051 + 052 are stubbed (audit-then-spec, not started).
